creator: arrowd@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Olivier Duchateau from comment #9)
I missed your shared-mime-info PR as well. Does that make me #2 in your worst committers list?

Jokes aside, feeling that you're being treated badly in the project doesn't allow you to insult other. Your options are:

- be more vocal and ping people asking them to review/commit your changes. O know it works, because I often push patches from Discord people and GitHub PRs. I agree, though, that this is suboptimal, but we're all volunteers and work in our free time.
- stop contributing to this project. I hope you won't go this route, as your patches are of high quality and very appreciated.

But don't insult other people, please, it is not constructive in any way and is plain rude.
